title: syringa tomentella subsp. sweginzowii
intro: Syringa tomentella subsp. sweginzowii, also known as the Amur lilac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Russian Far East and Northeast China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Syringa tomentella subsp. sweginzowii include its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches and body pain, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
